content: Contoso Ltd is a discrete manufacturer of automotive components headquartered in Detroit. In Q1 2026 they announced a $180M investment to modernize their supplier network and reduce inbound logistics volatility. CEO Jane Price cited supply-chain resilience as the #1 board priority.

Contoso Ltd signed a partnership with a large cloud provider in February 2026 to move ERP workloads to a hybrid architecture. The program is led by CIO Mark Hall and is scheduled to complete in 18 months.

Fabrikam Financial is a Tier-2 regional bank with 3,400 employees. Following a 2025 consent order, Fabrikam is investing heavily in KYC automation and back-office process re-engineering. CTO Priya Ramanathan announced in March 2026 that agentic-AI pilots will begin in claims and customer service.

Fabrikam Financial operates in a highly regulated environment with explicit data-residency obligations in the EU. Any AI rollout must run with customer-managed keys and private networking, and is subject to model-risk management review.

Adventure Works is a mid-market outdoor-goods retailer with 4,800 employees across North America. Head of Digital Sarah Lee announced a 2026 initiative to move from batch analytics to real-time propensity-to-buy models across their 230 storefronts and e-commerce site.
Adventure Works' buying committee typically includes the Head of Digital, VP Merchandising, and the CIO. Pilots are evaluated against a 90-day revenue-lift measurement bar.
